title: Rapper Flavor Flav donates sports equipment to Cambridge youth center
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Rapper Flavor Flav donated sports equipment to the Frisoli Youth Center in Cambridge, Massachusetts.
The donation included basketballs, volleyballs, soccer balls, tennis balls, and more to support youth programs.
The Frisoli Youth Center serves children in grades 4-8 and offers evening programs for teens aged 14-19.

title: Jaylen Brown has a candid answer when asked if he deserves NBA MVP this season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown averages a career-best 28.8 points, 7.0 rebounds, and 5.2 assists per game this season.
Boston Celtics are second in the Eastern Conference with a 54-26 record, despite Jayson Tatum's injury.
Brown acknowledges slim MVP odds, citing his leadership and contributions not fully captured by analytics.

title: Special World Cup ticket sales shatter Commuter Rail records in first day
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Over 17,450 special MBTA Commuter Rail tickets were sold on the first day for World Cup matches at Gillette Stadium.
The MBTA will operate 14 trains per match, expecting to handle up to 20,000 riders during the World Cup.
Boston Stadium World Cup rail tickets offer unlimited travel on match days, enhancing travel flexibility for attendees.

title: The Boston Fleet are ready to take a sold-out TD Garden by storm
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Fleet will play their first game at a sold-out TD Garden against the Montreal Victoire on Saturday.
Boston Fleet's Megan Keller, Aerin Frankel, and Haley Winn featured for their Olympic success during a Bruins game on March 31.
Boston's win streak extends to five games as they prepare for a clash of league leaders without their head coach present.

title: Fall River woman who led sex trafficking ring with 5 others pleads guilty
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Christy Parker, 28, pleaded guilty to leading an interstate sex trafficking ring involving two minors and one adult.
Parker and her co-defendants trafficked victims in Massachusetts, Rhode Island, and South Carolina in 2023.
She faces charges including conspiracy and sex trafficking of a minor, with potential life imprisonment and fines.

title: Police look to identify owners of 2 dogs found dead on South Shore
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Weymouth police seek owners of two dead dogs found on South Shore to provide closure.
A small white terrier-type dog was found dead near Whitmans Pond, wearing only a flea collar.
A suspiciously wrapped black and white Pitbull mix was discovered dead near Lakeshore Road.

title: She’s running Boston in memory of her son, and for  necrotizing enterocolitis
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Jennifer Canvasser, 42, from Davis, California, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon in memory of her son, Micah.
Canvasser founded the NEC Society after losing her son to necrotizing enterocolitis, aiming to protect families from the disease.
Following the marathon, Canvasser will present at the Pediatric Academic Societies Meeting in Boston on NEC care.

title: Braintree man sentenced to life in prison for murdering Salem State student 40 years ago
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: John Carey, 66, sentenced to life in prison for the 1986 murder of Salem State student Claire Gravel.
Carey was found guilty of first-degree murder based on DNA evidence recovered from Gravel's clothing.
The case was solved decades later, with a new lead in 2012 and Carey's indictment in 2022.

title: ‘It’s pure excitement’: What James Hagens said after first practice with Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens, 19, completed his first practice with the Boston Bruins and may play as soon as Saturday.
Coach Marco Sturm is cautious about Hagens's debut, focusing on his adjustment to NHL's demands and team dynamics.
Hagens's skating and playmaking skills could boost the Bruins' struggling power play and offensive depth this season.

title: Report: 76ers star Joel Embiid to undergo surgery for appendicitis
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Joel Embiid of the 76ers will undergo surgery for appendicitis and is ruled out for Thursday's game.
Philadelphia 76ers are currently seventh in the Eastern Conference with five regular-season games remaining.
Celtics could face 76ers in the playoffs, depending on the final seedings and outcome of the play-in tournament.
